title: String edge-case bugs: string-map returns (), string->number radix ignored
goal: Found by ESH-0053 string edge probing. Two R7RS string ops are broken: (1) (string-map char-upcase "abc") returns () instead of "ABC" — string-map does not build/return the mapped string (R7RS 6.7: string-map returns a string). (2) (string->number "ff" 16) returns () instead of 255 — the 2-arg radix form is ignored/unsupported (R7RS 6.2.6: (string->number string radix)). Confirmed CORRECT already: substring (start=end, full, OOB raises), string-ref, empty-string ops, string-reverse, char-upcase, number->string with radix, make-string fill, string-copy range, string-for-each.
next_actions: string-map: build and return a string from applying proc to each char (mirror string-for-each's traversal; multi-string variant if feasible). Currently yields ().
string->number: honor the optional radix argument (2/8/10/16) for the 2-arg form; (string->number "ff" 16) => 255, (string->number "1010" 2) => 10.
Add tests/string/string_edge_test.esk covering string-map (single + identity), string->number with each radix, and the already-correct cases as regression guards.
acceptance: string-for-each + other string ops unaffected; AOT + -r
campaign_payoff: Fixes two real R7RS string ops (string-map, string->number radix) needed for general correctness + SICP string exercises.
